They are competing in the [[2026 Super League season]], competed in the [[2026 Challenge Cup]] and, as Super League champions, also competed in the [[2026 World Club Challenge]]. ==Preseason friendlies== {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col3center col6center col9right" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!Report |- | 25 January; 15:00 | {{leagueicon|Wakefield|16}} [[Wakefield Trinity]] | H | [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] | {{WinDL|res=L}} | 6–34 | [[Bill Leyland|Leyland]] | [[Jack Charles (rugby league)|Charles]] (1/1) | | {{cite news |last1=Smailes |first1=James |title=Youthful Hull KR beaten by Wakefield Trinity as new signings get first run out |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/youthful-hull-kr-beaten-wakefield-10776220 |access-date=25 January 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=25 January 2026}} |} ==World Club Challenge== {{main|2026 World Club Challenge}} The [[2026 World Club Challenge]] was confirmed on 6 November 2025, and would be held at the [[MKM Stadium]]. The match was the first time since [[1986 Kangaroo tour of Great Britain and France#Test Venues|1986]] that Hull Kingston Rovers had played against an Australian team.{{cite news |title=World Club Challenge: Hull KR to host Brisbane Broncos as rugby league's battle for global supremacy returns in 2026 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13464377/world-club-challenge-hull-kr-to-host-brisbane-broncos-as-rugby-leagues-battle-for-global-supremacy-returns-in-2026 |access-date=6 November 2025 |work=Sky Sports |date=6 November 2025}} {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col3center col6center col9right" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV{{cite web |title=Fixtures |url=https://www.rugbyleagueontv.com/ |website=rugbyleagueontv.com |access-date=7 February 2026}}!!Report |- | 19 February, 19:30 | {{leagueicon|Brisbane|16}} [[Brisbane Broncos]] | H | [[MKM Stadium]] | {{WinDL|res=W}} | 30–24 | [[Tom Amone|Amone]], [[Elliot Minchella|Minchella]], [[Joe Burgess|Burgess]], [[Oliver Gildart|Gildart]], [[Peta Hiku|Hiku]] | [[Arthur Mourgue|Mourgue]] (2/2), [[Rhyse Martin|Martin]] (3/3) | 24,600 | [[BBC Three]]
[[Sky Sports]] | {{cite news |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=Hull KR secure legendary status with epic World Club Challenge triumph over Brisbane Broncos |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-secure-legendary-status-10825628 |access-date=19 February 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=19 February 2026}}{{cite news |last1=Brennan |first1=Stewart |title=Hull KR beat Brisbane to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c747nkewg09o |access-date=19 February 2026 |work=BBC Sport |date=19 February 2026}} |} ==Super League== {{main|2026 Super League season}} From 2026, the [[Super League]] expanded to 14 teams, with [[Toulouse Olympique]] and [[York Knights]] being promoted from the [[RFL Championship]] via the [[IMG (company)|IMG]] [[IMG Grading for the British Rugby Football League|Grading system]].{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=York and Toulouse to play in expanded Super League |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cly24zvxw3no |access-date=17 October 2025 |publisher=BBC Sport |date=17 October 2025}} At a meeting of all twelve Super League teams at the [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]] on 28 July 2025 to vote on the expansion proposal, Hull Kingston Rovers were one of two teams to vote against expanding the league, with the club preferring for expansion to have instead taken place ahead of the 2027 season.{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=The three clubs who did not vote for Super League expansion revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/the-three-clubs-who-were-against-super-league-expansion-revealed |access-date=10 September 2025 |publisher=Love Rugby League |date=28 July 2025}} On 14 May 2025, it was announced that Hull Kingston Rovers would be part of the 2026 [[Rugby League Las Vegas]] event, playing their Round 3 fixture against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at the [[Allegiant Stadium]] in [[Paradise, Nevada|Paradise]], [[Nevada]].{{cite news |title=Hull KR and Leeds Rhinos to meet in Las Vegas |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c62nz5gj0r7o |access-date=10 September 2025 |publisher=BBC Sport |date=14 May 2025}} ===Fixtures=== Hull Kingston Rovers' Super League 2026 fixtures were released on 27 November 2025:{{cite web |title=2026 Betfred Super League Fixtures Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] | A | [[BrewDog Stadium]] | | – | | | | [[Sky Sports]] | | |} ===Table=== {{2026 Super League regular season table|showteam=HKR}} ==Challenge Cup== {{main|2026 Challenge Cup}} Hull Kingston Rovers were first drawn on 12 January to play [[Castleford Lock Lane|Lock Lane ARLFC]] in Round 3, following Lock Lane's 42-6 defeat of [[Brighouse Rangers (1977)|Brighouse Rangers]] in their delayed Round 1 fixture on 17 January; as a result of [[Featherstone Rovers]] being removed from the competition, Lock Lane gained an automatic [[Bye (sports)|bye]] to enter Round 3.{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Challenge Cup Second Round confirmed as Hull KR's Third Round opponents locked in |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-latest-second-round-confirmed-in-full-2026 |access-date=19 January 2026 |work=Love Rugby League |date=18 January 2026}} The fixture was originally set to be played away at Lock Lane's [[Castleford Sports Stadium|Hicksons Arena]], with neighbouring [[Castleford Tigers]] offering their [[Wheldon Road]] stadium as an alternative venue, however both teams later agreed to swap the venue to Craven Park by the request of Lock Lane's players.{{cite news |last1=Shaw |first1=Matthew |title=Castleford Tigers respond as community club opt against using stadium for Challenge Cup clash |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-community-chllenge-cup |access-date=6 February 2026 |work=Love Rugby League |date=22 January 2026}} Following their victory against Lock Lane, which saw records for Hull Kingston Rovers' highest-scoring victory and most goals scored in a fixture broken,{{cite news |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=Hull KR set new club record as Challenge Cup title defence starts in emphatic fashion |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-set-new-club-10801068 |access-date=6 February 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=6 February 2026}} Hull Kingston Rovers beat the [[Huddersfield Giants]] away 52-12 in Round 4, entering them into the quarter-finals,{{cite news |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=Hull KR too strong for depleted Huddersfield Giants as Robins march into Challenge Cup quarter-finals |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-huddersfield-giants-cup-10867656 |access-date=14 March 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=14 March 2026}} then after beating the [[York Knights]] 48-10 at home,{{cite news |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=Hull KR hit back at York Knights shock to march into Challenge Cup semi-finals |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-hit-back-york-10911850 |access-date=11 April 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=11 April 2026}} were drawn to play [[2025 Challenge Cup final|2025 finalists]] [[Warrington Wolves]] in the semi-finals. A 32–12 victory over Catalans qualified Hull Kingston Rovers to play in the [[2026 Challenge Cup final]] at [[Wembley Stadium]], where they will play the [[Wigan Warriors]] in a [[2025 Super League Grand Final]] rematch.{{cite news |last1=Jackson |first1=William |title=Hull KR beat Warrington Wolves to book Challenge Cup final spot once more |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-beat-warrington-wolves-10958907 |access-date=10 May 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=10 May 2026}} Hull Kingston Rovers ultimately emerged as runners-up in the final against Wigan, who won the match 40-10 with a dominant display over Rovers amid hot conditions at Wembley.{{cite news |last1=Jackson |first1=William |title=Hull KR's grip on Challenge Cup ripped away by dominant Wigan Warriors in Wembley heat |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-krs-grip-challenge-cup-10991730 |access-date=30 May 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=30 May 2026}} {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col4center col7center col10right" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Round!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV!!Report |- | 6 February;
